description:
## Goal

Refactor `tests/fixture_matrix.rs` so each `(fixture, path)` pair is its own trial in `cargo test` output, instead of all collapsing into one `test fixture_matrix ... ok` line. Failures will then point at exactly which fixture × leg diverged, and `cargo test add1` / `cargo test ::repl` / etc. will filter naturally.

## Approach

Use the `libtest-mimic` crate (small, purpose-built for runtime-discovered tests).

### Cargo.toml
- Add `libtest-mimic = "0.8"` (or current) as a dev-dependency.
- Add `[[test]] name = "fixture_matrix" harness = false` so the file owns its own `fn main`.

### tests/fixture_matrix.rs
- Keep `discover`, `parse_header_from_dir`, `run_path`, `run_aot_path`, `run_repl_path`, `normalize`, comparison logic — all reusable.
- Replace the top-level `#[test] fn fixture_matrix()` with `fn main()` that:
  1. Calls `discover()` to get fixtures.
  2. For each fixture, parses the header and builds one `Trial::test(...)` per declared path, named `"<fixture>::<path>"`.
  3. Calls `libtest_mimic::run(&Arguments::from_args(), trials).exit()`.
- Each trial closure runs `run_path` and compares to `expected.txt`. Deferred → trial reports as ignored with the reason. Failed → trial fails with the diff at `<dir>/actual.txt`.
- `fixture_index_up_to_date` becomes its own `Trial::test("fixture_index_up_to_date", ...)` so `BLESS=1` still works.

## Acceptance

- `cargo test --test fixture_matrix` lists each fixture × path on its own line: `test add1::jit ... ok`, `test add1::interp ... ok`, `test add1::repl ... ok`, `test alias::jit ... ok`, ….
- `cargo test --test fixture_matrix add1` runs only add1's legs.
- `cargo test --test fixture_matrix ::repl` runs every REPL leg.
- A made-up failing case shows exactly which `<fixture>::<path>` failed and dumps `actual.txt`.
- `BLESS=1 cargo test --test fixture_matrix fixture_index_up_to_date` still works.
- `cargo test --all` green; clippy `-D warnings` clean.

## Non-changes

- No fixture frontmatter changes. No new opt-ins.
- No change to what's actually executed — same backends, same comparisons. Purely test-harness UX.
title: fixture matrix: one trial per (fixture, path) via libtest-mimic
